items.title: Regenerate WordPress Security Keys and Salts
items.description: Replace default AUTH_KEY, SECURE_AUTH_KEY, LOGGED_IN_KEY, NONCE_KEY and corresponding salts with unique values.
items.verification: All AUTH_KEY, SECURE_AUTH_KEY, LOGGED_IN_KEY, NONCE_KEY are unique and not default/empty

items.title: Set Secure File and Directory Permissions
items.description: Files should be 644, directories 755. wp-config.php should be 600 or 440.
items.verification: ls -la shows 644 for files, 755 for dirs, 600 for wp-config.php

items.title: Password Protect wp-admin Directory
items.description: Add HTTP Basic Authentication to wp-admin to prevent unauthorized access even if WordPress auth is bypassed.

implementation.apache: htpasswd -c /etc/apache2/.htpasswd admin; Add AuthType Basic in wp-admin/.htaccess
implementation.nginx: Use nginx auth_basic; point to htpasswd file in location block

items.verification: Accessing /wp-admin prompts for HTTP Basic Auth credentials before WordPress login

items.title: Disable XML-RPC if Not Needed
items.description: XML-RPC is a common attack vector for brute force amplification and pingback DDoS. Disable if unused.

implementation.apache: Add to .htaccess: Files xmlrpc.php -> deny from all; OR use plugin

items.verification: POST to /xmlrpc.php returns 403 Forbidden or 404 Not Found

items.title: Restrict REST API Access
items.description: Limit REST API endpoints to authenticated users or specific IP ranges. Exposes user data by default.

implementation.apache: Use plugin or add RewriteRule to block /wp-json/wp/v2/users

items.title: Change Default Database Table Prefix
items.description: Change wp_ to a random prefix during install or via migration to prevent SQLi enumeration.

implementation.apache: Edit wp-config.php: table_prefix = 'xyz123_'; rename all DB tables
implementation.nginx: Same process - edit wp-config.php and rename tables

items.verification: SHOW TABLES shows custom prefix, no tables starting with wp_

items.title: Disable WP_DEBUG and Error Display
items.description: Debug mode exposes paths, database names, and stack traces that aid attackers.

implementation.apache: wp-config.php: define('WP_DEBUG', false); define('WP_DEBUG_DISPLAY', false); ini_set('display_errors', 0);

items.verification: Intentionally trigger an error; generic message shown, no path disclosure

items.title: Disable Theme and Plugin File Editor
items.description: Disallow editing files through WordPress admin to limit damage from compromised accounts.
items.verification: Appearance > Editor and Plugins > Editor show disabled message

items.title: Force HTTPS/SSL for All Pages
items.description: Redirect HTTP to HTTPS and enable HSTS. Set FORCE_SSL_ADMIN in wp-config.php.
items.verification: Access http:// redirects to https://; admin login enforces SSL

items.title: Add HTTP Security Headers
items.description: Configure X-Frame-Options, X-XSS-Protection, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, and CSP.

implementation.apache: Header set X-Frame-Options SAMEORIGIN; Header set X-Content-Type-Options nosniff; Header set Referrer-Policy
implementation.nginx: add_header X-Frame-Options SAMEORIGIN; add_header X-Content-Type-Options nosniff

items.verification: curl -I shows all security headers present

items.title: Implement Automated Backups
items.description: Automate daily backups of database and files using UpdraftPlus or WP CLI cron jobs.

implementation.apache: Install UpdraftPlus plugin and schedule daily backups to remote storage
implementation.nginx: Same plugin setup via WordPress admin

items.verification: Backup files exist with recent timestamps in configured storage

items.title: Audit and Remove Unused Plugins
items.description: Deactivate and delete unused plugins. Keep all active plugins updated. Check for known vulnerabilities.

implementation.apache: WordPress admin: Plugins > Installed Plugins > delete inactive plugins
implementation.linux: wp plugin list --status=inactive --path=/var/www/html; wp plugin delete inactive-slug

items.verification: Only required plugins are active and updated to latest version

items.title: Remove Unused Themes and Child Theme Security
items.description: Delete unused themes, especially default WordPress themes. Keep parent and child themes updated.
items.verification: Only active theme (and parent if child) remains in wp-content/themes

items.title: Enable Two-Factor Authentication
items.description: Require 2FA for administrator accounts using plugins like Wordfence or Two Factor.

implementation.apache: Install Two Factor or Wordfence Login Security plugin; configure TOTP for admin role
implementation.nginx: Same plugin setup via WordPress admin

items.verification: Admin login requires TOTP code or backup code after password

items.title: Change Default Login URL
items.description: Obfuscate /wp-login.php and /wp-admin to reduce brute force attack surface.

implementation.apache: Install WPS Hide Login or Change wp-admin Login plugin; set custom slug

items.verification: Accessing /wp-login.php redirects to 404; new custom URL works for login

items.title: Hide WordPress Version Number
items.description: Remove WordPress version from RSS feeds and meta generator to prevent targeted attacks.
items.verification: View page source does not contain meta generator WordPress version tag

items.title: Prevent Execution of PHP in Uploads Directory
items.description: Uploaded files in /wp-content/uploads should never be executable as PHP.

implementation.apache: Add .htaccess in uploads: php_flag engine off; or FilesMatch deny all for .php

items.verification: Upload PHP file to uploads; direct access returns 403

items.title: Implement Login Attempt Limiting
items.description: Block repeated failed login attempts to prevent brute force and credential stuffing.

implementation.apache: Install Wordfence or Limit Login Attempts Reloaded plugin; set to 3 retries then 1 hour lockout
implementation.nginx: Same plugin approach or nginx limit_req

items.verification: 3 failed logins trigger lockout message and email notification
